a_title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
b_title: Score-Based Generative Modeling through Stochastic Differential Equations
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues that while DDPM (Ho et al., 2020) was reported to achieve higher sample quality than SMLD (Song & Ermon, 2019; 2020), their framework with better architectures and new sampling algorithms allows SMLD to catch up and achieve a new state-of-the-art Inception score, indicating a disagreement on the superiority of DDPM.

a_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
b_title: Tokens-to-Token ViT: Training Vision Transformers from Scratch on ImageNet
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a disagreement with the Vision Transformer (ViT) by proposing a new model, T2T-ViT, which outperforms ViT in terms of accuracy and efficiency, indicating that the authors believe their approach is superior.

a_title: A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations
b_title: Understanding Contrastive Representation Learning through Alignment and Uniformity on the Hypersphere
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a significant inconsistency between theoretical results suggesting that representation quality suffers with a large number of negatives and empirical observations that show the opposite, indicating a strong disagreement with the theoretical claims.

a_title: Bootstrap Your Own Latent: A New Approach to Self-Supervised Learning
b_title: Exploring Simple Siamese Representation Learning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the necessity of a large-batch optimizer like LARS, stating that their method does not require it, contrasting with the findings of previous works [8, 15, 7].

a_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
b_title: Prompt Programming for Large Language Models: Beyond the Few-Shot Paradigm
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues against the common interpretation that GPT-3 learns from few-shot examples at runtime, stating that it often does not actually learn the task during runtime.

a_title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
b_title: SDEdit: Guided Image Synthesis and Editing with Stochastic Differential Equations
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights that SDEdit does not require the extensive dataset collection and retraining needed for conditional GANs, indicating a disagreement with the necessity of these processes in GANs.

a_title: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
b_title: BEiT: BERT Pre-Training of Image Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper disagrees with the approach of using pixel-level auto-encoding for vision pre-training, arguing that it focuses too much on short-range dependencies and high-frequency details, which is seen as a limitation compared to their proposed method using discrete VAE.

a_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
b_title: ViLT: Vision-and-Language Transformer Without Convolution or Region Supervision
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses strong disagreement with the performance of CLIP on vision-and-language downstream tasks, specifically noting that fine-tuning on NLVR2 resulted in low accuracy, indicating that CLIP's representations are inadequate for this task.

a_title: Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems
b_title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with prior approaches that rely on additional verifiers or re-rankers, arguing that self-consistency is simpler and more effective without the need for task-specific training.

a_title: End-to-End Object Detection with Transformers
b_title: Unsupervised Learning of Visual Features by Contrasting Cluster Assignments
summary_of_disagreement: The paper suggests a disagreement with the approach of Faster R-CNN, highlighting that, unlike Faster R-CNN, using a pretrained backbone in DETR is crucial for obtaining good results, implying that Faster R-CNN does not rely as heavily on pretrained models for performance.

a_title: Unsupervised Learning of Visual Features by Contrasting Cluster Assignments
b_title: Exploring Simple Siamese Representation Learning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that their method does not require a large-batch optimizer like LARS, which contrasts with the methods referenced that do require such optimizers.

a_title: Informer: Beyond Efficient Transformer for Long Sequence Time-Series Forecasting
b_title: Autoformer: Decomposition Transformers with Auto-Correlation for Long-Term Series Forecasting
summary_of_disagreement: The paper suggests that pre-decomposing may have negative effects by neglecting the interaction of components during long-term forecasting, which contrasts with the approach taken by some Transformer-based models that focus on self-attention improvements.

a_title: Deformable DETR: Deformable Transformers for End-to-End Object Detection
b_title: Transformers in Vision: A Survey
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights that while DETR struggles with small object detection and has slow convergence, D-DETR significantly improves upon these issues, indicating a clear disagreement on the effectiveness of DETR compared to D-DETR.

a_title: Training data-efficient image transformers & distillation through attention
b_title: CvT: Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement with other prior Transformer-based architectures by stating that they do not sum the ad-hoc position embedding to the tokens, which contrasts with the methods used by those architectures.

a_title: OPT: Open Pre-trained Transformer Language Models
b_title: BLOOM: A 176B-Parameter Open-Access Multilingual Language Model
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that the carbon footprint estimate of the OPT model is roughly twice as high as the estimate provided in the quoted research paper, suggesting a significant disagreement on the environmental impact of the models.

a_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
b_title: Muse: Text-To-Image Generation via Masked Generative Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the performance of Stable Diffusion compared to Muse, stating that Muse is significantly better aligned and faster than Stable Diffusion.

a_title: BLIP-2: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training with Frozen Image Encoders and Large Language Models
b_title: MiniGPT-4: Enhancing Vision-Language Understanding with Advanced Large Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues that previous models like Kosmos-1 and BLIP-2 lack certain advanced abilities due to their use of less powerful language models, implying a strong disagreement with the effectiveness of these models compared to MiniGPT-4.

a_title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
b_title: Visual Prompt Tuning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a contrast between the performance of Visual Prompt Tuning (VPT) in vision tasks and prompt tuning in NLP, indicating that VPT can exceed the performance of full fine-tuning, which is not the case in NLP where prompt tuning only matches full fine-tuning under certain conditions.

a_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
b_title: WizardLM: Empowering Large Language Models to Follow Complex Instructions
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the performance of Vicuna, stating that their model WizardLM significantly outperforms Vicuna when fine-tuned with the same amount of data.

a_title: Exploring Simple Siamese Representation Learning
b_title: VICReg: Variance-Invariance-Covariance Regularization for Self-Supervised Learning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with previous methods by stating that their proposed architecture is symmetric and does not require the stop-gradient operation of SimSiam or the momentum encoder of BYOL, which contrasts with the reliance on these mechanisms in those methods.

a_title: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
b_title: Language Models as Zero-Shot Planners: Extracting Actionable Knowledge for Embodied Agents
summary_of_disagreement: The paper disagrees with Chen et al. (2021) by stating that unlike their approach which allows for selecting the sample with the highest unit test pass rate, the current study only evaluates one sample per task.

a_title: LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications
b_title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper disagrees with previous approaches that require additional re-rankers or verifiers for improving generation quality, asserting that self-consistency is simpler and more efficient.

a_title: TransUNet: Transformers Make Strong Encoders for Medical Image Segmentation
b_title: Swin-Unet: Unet-like Pure Transformer for Medical Image Segmentation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement with the effectiveness of CNN-based approaches in learning global and long-range semantic information, contrasting with the methods proposed in the referenced paper [2].

a_title: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis & Insights from Training Gopher
b_title: A Survey of Large Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a disagreement regarding the effectiveness of classifier-based approaches for text quality filtering, noting that they may inadvertently remove high-quality texts from dialectal and colloquial languages, which could introduce bias.

a_title: Image Super-Resolution via Iterative Refinement
b_title: Palette: Image-to-Image Diffusion Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues that while GANs have been widely used and are capable of generating high fidelity outputs, they often require auxiliary objectives and lack diversity in their outputs, which contrasts with the performance of diffusion models that have shown impressive results in specific applications like image super-resolution.

a_title: Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models
b_title: Fast Sampling of Diffusion Models with Exponential Integrator
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement regarding the performance of deterministic versus stochastic DDIM, noting that the deterministic DDIM outperforms the stochastic version, which contrasts with the findings of Song et al. (2020a).

a_title: On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models
b_title: Visual Prompt Tuning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a contrast between the performance of prompt tuning in NLP and visual prompting, indicating that visual prompting can exceed the performance of prompt tuning under certain conditions, which is not the case in NLP.

a_title: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
b_title: Survey of Hallucination in Natural Language Generation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the effectiveness of traditional automatic evaluation metrics like ROUGE and F1 for measuring hallucination in GQA, stating they have poor correlation with human judgments.

a_title: Improved Baselines with Momentum Contrastive Learning
b_title: Understanding Contrastive Representation Learning through Alignment and Uniformity on the Hypersphere
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a strong disagreement regarding the impact of a large number of negatives on representation quality. It contrasts the theoretical insights of Saunshi et al. (2019), which suggest representation quality suffers with a large number of negatives, against empirical observations from Wu et al. (2018), Tian et al. (2019), He et al. (2019), and Chen et al. (2020) that demonstrate otherwise.

a_title: Transformers in Vision: A Survey
b_title: A Survey of Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper discusses the instability in training post-LN Transformers compared to pre-LN Transformers, suggesting that the gradient issues and output shifts in post-LN Transformers lead to unstable training, which is not a problem for pre-LN Transformers.

a_title: Visual Instruction Tuning
b_title: InstructBLIP: Towards General-purpose Vision-Language Models with Instruction Tuning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the performance of LLaVA and MiniGPT-4, stating that they generate long and less relevant sentences compared to InstructBLIP, which adapts the response length to better address user intent.

a_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
b_title: Co-Scale Conv-Attentional Image Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows a clear disagreement with the performance of Swin Transformer models by demonstrating that their CoaT and CoaT-Lite models have clear performance advantages over Swin Transformers in terms of accuracy under both the 1x and 3x settings, despite having larger latency/FLOPs.

a_title: Mip-NeRF: A Multiscale Representation for Anti-Aliasing Neural Radiance Fields
b_title: Mip-NeRF 360: Unbounded Anti-Aliased Neural Radiance Fields
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with mip-NeRF's approach, arguing that its axis-aligned IPE features fail to capture the covariance of the Gaussian being encoded, leading to identical features for different distributions, while the proposed off-axis IPE features allow for disambiguation.

a_title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
b_title: AdaptFormer: Adapting Vision Transformers for Scalable Visual Recognition
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights a disagreement regarding the scaling factor used in fine-tuning between NLP and vision tasks, stating that while NLP prefers a scaling factor greater than 1, the authors found that a scaling factor of 1 is necessary for stability in vision tasks.

a_title: Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents
b_title: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement with the previous dominance of generative adversarial networks (GANs) by stating that diffusion models and score-based generative models have outperformed GANs in image generation.

a_title: Do As I Can, Not As I Say: Grounding Language in Robotic Affordances
b_title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the effectiveness of the SayCan algorithm (Ahn et al., 2022) and its use of oracle affordance functions, stating that these functions are insufficient for constructing long-horizon plans in TAMP environments.

a_title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
b_title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
summary_of_disagreement: The paper contrasts its findings with those of Ouyang et al. (2022), suggesting a different perspective on the concept of 'alignment tax' in few-shot evaluations.

a_title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners
b_title: MetaICL: Learning to Learn In Context
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that its findings somewhat contradict those of Wei et al. (2022) and Sanh et al. (2022), particularly regarding the performance of multi-task learning and model size requirements.

a_title: The Power of Scale for Parameter-Efficient Prompt Tuning
b_title: P-Tuning v2: Prompt Tuning Can Be Comparable to Fine-tuning Universally Across Scales and Tasks
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that prompt tuning underperforms compared to fine-tuning for models with less than 10 billion parameters, which contradicts the notion that prompt tuning is always effective.

a_title: Large Language Models are Zero-Shot Reasoners
b_title: Automatic Chain of Thought Prompting in Large Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that Manual-CoT has obtained stronger performance than Zero-Shot-CoT, suggesting a disagreement with the effectiveness of Zero-Shot-CoT as presented by Kojima et al. (2022).

a_title: GPT-4 Technical Report
b_title: LLaMA-Adapter: Efficient Fine-tuning of Language Models with Zero-init Attention
summary_of_disagreement: The paper suggests that despite the GPT series having more parameters, the LLaMA 7B model with lightweight adapters performs comparably, indicating a disagreement on the necessity of larger models for effective performance.

a_title: DreamFusion: Text-to-3D using 2D Diffusion
b_title: Fantasia3D: Disentangling Geometry and Appearance for High-quality Text-to-3D Content Creation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses strong disagreement with DreamFusion's methods and results, highlighting its own superior performance in appearance and geometry generation, ability to modify materials which DreamFusion struggles with, and the claim that DreamFusion's coupling of geometry and appearance learning is suboptimal compared to their approach.

a_title: PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways
b_title: Is ChatGPT a General-Purpose Natural Language Processing Task Solver?
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that ChatGPT performs worse than PaLM, suggesting a disagreement with the effectiveness of ChatGPT compared to other models.

a_title: Score-Based Generative Modeling through Stochastic Differential Equations
b_title: Diffusion Schrödinger Bridge with Applications to Score-Based Generative Modeling
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses a disagreement with Song et al. (2021) by stating a contrary position on the consideration of time homogeneous diffusions.

a_title: A ConvNet for the 2020s
b_title: Visual attention network
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that VAN-B2 surpasses ConvNeXt in performance, suggesting a disagreement with the effectiveness of ConvNeXt compared to VAN.

a_title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
b_title: GLM-130B: An Open Bilingual Pre-trained Model
summary_of_disagreement: The paper suggests that existing MIP paradigms may lead to stronger zero-shot learning but weaker in-context few-shot performance, indicating a critique of those paradigms compared to the proposed approach.

a_title: Chain of Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
b_title: STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that while few-shot techniques with rationales generally outperform non-reasoning counterparts, they still underperform compared to models fine-tuned on larger datasets, suggesting a disagreement with the effectiveness of few-shot prompting alone as presented in previous works.

a_title: Instant neural graphics primitives with a multiresolution hash encoding
b_title: DreamGaussian: Generative Gaussian Splatting for Efficient 3D Content Creation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues that the occupancy pruning technique used to accelerate NeRF is ineffective in generative settings when supervised by ambiguous SDS loss, contrasting with its effectiveness in reconstruction settings.

a_title: Is Space-Time Attention All You Need for Video Understanding?
b_title: ViViT: A Video Vision Transformer
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the approach taken by the concurrent work [4], particularly highlighting that their Factorised Encoder model significantly outperforms [4]'s TimeSformer method by 2.9%, emphasizing the superiority of their method over the pure-transformer model proposed by [4] which did not consider the Factorised encoder variant or additional regularisation.

a_title: SegFormer: Simple and Efficient Design for Semantic Segmentation with Transformers
b_title: SegNeXt: Rethinking Convolutional Attention Design for Semantic Segmentation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the performance of transformer-based models like SegFormer and HRFormer, asserting that their convolutional model SegNeXt outperforms these state-of-the-art transformer-based methods.

a_title: Scaling Up Visual and Vision-Language Representation Learning With Noisy Text Supervision
b_title: Open-vocabulary Object Detection via Vision and Language Knowledge Distillation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that the approach works well with ALIGN but does not perform well with CLIP, suggesting a disagreement in effectiveness between the two models.

a_title: Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions
b_title: The Flan Collection: Designing Data and Methods for Effective Instruction Tuning
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that Super-Natural Instructions has limited scaling benefits on Held-Out task performance, which contrasts with the findings of other studies that may suggest otherwise.

a_title: MLP-Mixer: An all-MLP Architecture for Vision
b_title: Visual attention network
summary_of_disagreement: The paper highlights drawbacks of gMLP, specifically its limitations in processing fixed-size images and ignoring local structures, suggesting a disagreement with its effectiveness compared to other models like VAN.

a_title: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
b_title: Improving alignment of dialogue agents via targeted human judgements
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents strong disagreement with Bai et al. (2022) regarding the methods used for reinforcement learning and the handling of alignment tax, emphasizing a different approach through self-play and fine-grained rules.

a_title: Rethinking Semantic Segmentation from a Sequence-to-Sequence Perspective with Transformers
b_title: TransFuse: Fusing Transformers and CNNs for Medical Image Segmentation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the effectiveness of SETR-based segmentation networks, stating that they produce unsatisfactory performance due to a lack of spatial inductive bias and the destruction of low-level features.

a_title: Supervised Contrastive Learning
b_title: Out-of-distribution Detection with Deep Nearest Neighbors
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a disagreement with the findings of Ming et al. (2022b), which propose a prototype-based contrastive learning framework that claims to achieve stronger ID-OOD separability than the SupCon loss used in the current study.

a_title: BEiT: BERT Pre-Training of Image Transformers
b_title: Masked Feature Prediction for Self-Supervised Visual Pre-Training
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with BEiT by highlighting that, unlike BEiT which uses a dVAE tokenizer, MaskFeat eliminates the need for this tokenizer, thereby avoiding an extra pre-training stage and reducing inference overhead.

a_title: Oscar: Object-Semantics Aligned Pre-training for Vision-Language Tasks
b_title: Align before Fuse: Vision and Language Representation Learning with Momentum Distillation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the necessity of using pre-trained object detectors for extracting region-based image features in multimodal encoders, highlighting ALBEF's advantage in not requiring such detectors and thus avoiding a major computational bottleneck present in existing methods.

a_title: The Pile: An 800GB Dataset of Diverse Text for Language Modeling
b_title: The RefinedWeb Dataset for Falcon LLM: Outperforming Curated Corpora with Web Data, and Web Data Only
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses disagreement with the use of WET files, stating they include undesirable elements like navigation menus and ads, which contrasts with the preference for curated datasets like The Pile.

a_title: BLIP: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training for Unified Vision-Language Understanding and Generation
b_title: Evaluating Object Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that InstructBLIP outperforms other models like LLaVA, MultiModal-GPT, and mPLUG-Owl, which suffer from severe hallucination issues, suggesting a disagreement on the effectiveness of these models compared to InstructBLIP.

a_title: Photorealistic Text-to-Image Diffusion Models with Deep Language Understanding
b_title: Extracting Training Data from Diffusion Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper disagrees with the assertion made by previous researchers that overfitting is not an issue in large-scale training efforts, specifically highlighting that despite these claims, their findings show that models like Imagen do memorize individual examples, indicating a problem with overfitting.

a_title: SwinIR: Image Restoration Using Swin Transformer
b_title: Vision Transformers for Single Image Dehazing
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the effectiveness of GELU in low-level vision tasks, particularly in image dehazing, where it is found to perform worse than ReLU and LeakyReLU. It argues that GELU's non-monotonicity and complexity lead to inferior performance in this context.

a_title: Video Diffusion Models
b_title: Make-A-Video: Text-to-Video Generation without Text-Video Data
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with VDM (Ho et al., 2022) by highlighting that their use of pseudo-3D convolution and temporal attention layers allows for better temporal information fusion compared to VDM, suggesting a superior approach in handling video and temporal data.

a_title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
b_title: Scaling Laws for Autoregressive Generative Modeling
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement with the findings of [KMH20] regarding the optimal aspect ratio for different domains, suggesting that math, images, and videos prefer much deeper models compared to language, where [KMH20] suggests a different optimal aspect ratio.

a_title: Taming Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis
b_title: Vector-quantized Image Modeling with Improved VQGAN
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with VQGAN (Esser et al., 2021) by showcasing their method's superior performance in image synthesis without relying on top-k and top-p (nucleus) sampling heuristics, achieving significantly better FID scores on CelebAHQ and FFHQ.

a_title: Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners
b_title: Label-Efficient Semantic Segmentation with Diffusion Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper argues that the proposed DDPM-based method is more robust and superior to the MAE and SwAV models, indicating a disagreement with the effectiveness of these self-supervised methods.

a_title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
b_title: GIT: A Generative Image-to-text Transformer for Vision and Language
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates disagreement with the findings of Flamingo (Alayrac et al., 2022) by claiming to achieve higher accuracy on certain tasks while performing worse on others, suggesting a critique of Flamingo's approach.

a_title: Pyramid Vision Transformer: A Versatile Backbone for Dense Prediction without Convolutions
b_title: Multi-Scale Vision Longformer: A New Vision Transformer for High-Resolution Image Encoding
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates a disagreement with the reduction ratios used in PVT [43], stating that their multi-scale SRA model employs different ratios (16, 8, 4, 2) compared to PVT's (8, 4, 2, 1).

a_title: COVID-Net: a tailored deep convolutional neural network design for detection of COVID-19 cases from chest X-ray images
b_title: COVID-ResNet: A Deep Learning Framework for Screening of COVID19 from Radiographs
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates disagreement with the methodology of the referenced study [7], particularly in the use of data augmentation techniques which they claim led to significant performance improvements not achieved by the authors of [7].

a_title: Are Transformers Effective for Time Series Forecasting?
b_title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with the findings of Zeng et al. (2022), challenging the usefulness of Transformer-based models for time series forecasting by showing that a simple linear model can outperform these more complex models on common benchmarks. This challenges the prevailing notion of the superiority of Transformer models in this domain.

a_title: Training Generative Adversarial Networks with Limited Data
b_title: StyleGAN-XL: Scaling StyleGAN to Large Diverse Datasets
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses disagreement with the effectiveness of BigGAN compared to StyleGAN, noting that BigGAN's performance varies significantly between training runs, while StyleGAN maintains a higher standing.

a_title: wav2vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations
b_title: Pushing the Limits of Semi-Supervised Learning for Automatic Speech Recognition
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that their pre-training procedure differs from that of the referenced paper [6] in several ways, suggesting a disagreement with the methods or conclusions presented in that paper.

a_title: Conformer: Convolution-augmented Transformer for Speech Recognition
b_title: Emotion Recognition from Speech Using Wav2vec 2.0 Embeddings
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the notion that finetuning the wav2vec 2.0 model for ASR improves performance for emotion recognition, indicating that finetuning eliminates useful information from the embeddings.

a_title: LightGCN: Simplifying and Powering Graph Convolution Network for Recommendation
b_title: Disentangled Graph Collaborative Filtering
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with prior works [10, 36, 40] by arguing against the approach of treating a user-item interaction as an isolated data instance or uniformly organizing it as an edge in the interaction graph. It also criticizes the use of nonlinear transformation adopted by prior works and proposes establishing a set of intent-aware graphs instead of a uniform one, indicating a move away from the methodologies used in works like LightGCN [10], which it views as a special case of its p...

a_title: GLIDE: Towards Photorealistic Image Generation and Editing with Text-Guided Diffusion Models
b_title: DiffEdit: Diffusion-based semantic image editing with mask guidance
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with the technique used by Nichol et al. (2021) in GLIDE, specifically criticizing their operator for interpolating images. The authors argue that their mask guidance operator provides a better trade-off, evidenced by achieving a lower CSFID score, indicating superior performance in their approach.

a_title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
b_title: LongBench: A Bilingual, Multitask Benchmark for Long Context Understanding
summary_of_disagreement: The paper indicates that the performance of Llama2-7B-chat-4k is inferior to that of 5-Turbo-16k and ChatGLM2-6B-32k, suggesting a disagreement with the effectiveness of Llama2-7B-chat-4k compared to the other models.

a_title: Prompt-to-Prompt Image Editing with Cross Attention Control
b_title: Plug-and-Play Diffusion Features for Text-Driven Image-to-Image Translation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with the P2P method, particularly criticizing its inability to deviate from the guidance appearance to satisfy edits on real images and its requirement for word-to-word alignment between source and target text prompts, which limits its flexibility and effectiveness in text-guided image-to-image tasks.

a_title: Plenoxels: Radiance Fields without Neural Networks
b_title: TensoRF: Tensorial Radiance Fields
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with concurrent works [46, 25] and Plenoxels [46] by highlighting that, unlike these works which require unique data structures and customized CUDA kernels, their model achieves efficiency gains using a standard PyTorch implementation. Additionally, it claims better quality, faster or comparable reconstruction speed, and smaller model sizes compared to Plenoxels [46].

a_title: Swin Transformer V2: Scaling Up Capacity and Resolution
b_title: EVA: Exploring the Limits of Masked Visual Representation Learning at Scale
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a strong disagreement with the approach of SwinV2-Giant and FD-SwinV2Giant, criticizing their reliance on a larger model size and a stronger HTC++ detector. It highlights its own method's efficiency by streamlining pre-training processes and achieving better representations without the need for significantly larger model sizes, describing this achievement as a "Giant-killing" act.

a_title: pixelNeRF: Neural Radiance Fields from One or Few Images
b_title: Point-NeRF: Point-based Neural Radiance Fields
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the efficiency and accuracy of PixelNeRF, IBRNet, and other neural radiance field models, stating that Point-NeRF leads to more efficient reconstruction and more accurate rendering. It also claims higher rendering quality and faster radiance field reconstruction than PixelNeRF and IBRNet, and significantly better initial radiance field estimation than PixelNeRF.

a_title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
b_title: Extending Context Window of Large Language Models via Positional Interpolation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses disagreement with Su et al. (2021) regarding the tightness of the extrapolation bound derived in Section 3.4.3. It suggests that the bound may be too loose, indicating that while it decays with respect to the positional difference, it can still be quite large and potentially vacuous, challenging the effectiveness of the bound in practical scenarios.

a_title: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer
b_title: Multi-Scale Vision Longformer: A New Vision Transformer for High-Resolution Image Encoding
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with the original Longformer [2] regarding the separation of attention parameters for global and local tokens. The authors did not observe any gain from this separation in their experiments, contrary to what was suggested in [2].

a_title: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual–Inertial, and Multimap SLAM
b_title: DROID-SLAM: Deep Visual SLAM for Monocular, Stereo, and RGB-D Cameras
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows strong disagreement with the performance of ORB-SLAM3, highlighting a significant reduction in error rates when comparing their method to ORB-SLAM3. Specifically, they claim to reduce error by 82% among methods with zero failures and by 43% over ORB-SLAM3 in certain conditions, and by 71% with stereo input.

a_title: Uformer: A General U-Shaped Transformer for Image Restoration
b_title: Vision Transformers for Single Image Dehazing
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the common use of GELU in vision Transformers for low-level vision tasks like image dehazing, arguing that GELU harms performance due to its non-monotonicity and difficulty to be inverted, which is critical for such tasks. It suggests that ReLU and LeakyReLU perform better than GELU for image dehazing.

a_title: CvT: Introducing Convolutions to Vision Transformers
b_title: Conditional Positional Encodings for Vision Transformers
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a disagreement with CvT by highlighting its method's superiority in learning better position encodings and achieving higher accuracy on the ImageNet validation set. It criticizes CvT's use of depth-wise convolution in q-k-v projection for having more parameters and still resulting in lower performance compared to their method.

a_title: NeRF
b_title: Free View Synthesis
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows a strong disagreement with the methods published concurrently, specifically Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) [27] and Neural Point-Based Graphics (NPBG) [2], by claiming to convincingly outperform these methods.

a_title: Improved Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
b_title: Diffusion Schrödinger Bridge with Applications to Score-Based Generative Modeling
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with the architecture of Song and Ermon (2020) by stating that using it resulted in worse results within their framework.

a_title: Multi-Stage Progressive Image Restoration
b_title: MAXIM: Multi-Axis MLP for Image Processing
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses a strong disagreement with the multi-patch hierarchy approach [81, 108, 110], favoring full resolution processing [15, 67, 75] instead, due to potential boundary effects induced by the former.

a_title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
b_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with Hoffmann et al. (2022) by demonstrating that their reasoning token approach outperforms Chinchilla on mathematical MMLU, suggesting that the focus on increasing tokens indefinitely (as proposed by Chinchilla) should be complemented with strong data quality procedures.

a_title: CoCa: Contrastive Captioners are Image-Text Foundation Models
b_title: Scaling Vision Transformers to 22 Billion Parameters
summary_of_disagreement: The paper presents a disagreement with CoCa's approach by highlighting the contrast in methodology, specifically in terms of using one token per image patch for video classification and the resolution used in their experiments. It also contrasts CoCa's combination of contrastive and generative caption pretraining with its own supervised pretraining approach.

a_title: ViLT: Vision-and-Language Transformer Without Convolution or Region Supervision
b_title: LayoutLMv3: Pre-training for Document AI with Unified Text and Image Masking
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with the approach taken by ViLT and METER in leveraging MPM similar to ViT and BEiT for reconstructing visual elements, stating it resulted in degraded performance on downstream tasks.

a_title: GLM-130B: An Open Bilingual Pre-trained Model
b_title: A Comprehensive Overview of Large Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement regarding the effectiveness of positional encoding methods for GLM-130B, where it contrasts the model's identification of rotary positional encoding as better than ALiBi, indicating a divergence in findings or approaches.

a_title: Sharpness-Aware Minimization for Efficiently Improving Generalization
b_title: SWAD: Domain Generalization by Seeking Flat Minima
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows a disagreement with the effectiveness of sharpness-aware minimization (SAM) compared to Stochastic Weight Averaging (SWA), stating that SWA finds flatter minima than SAM, which implies a critique of SAM's performance in finding the most desirable minima for generalization.

a_title: CoAtNet: Marrying Convolution and Attention for All Data Sizes
b_title: MobileViT: Light-weight, General-purpose, and Mobile-friendly Vision Transformer
summary_of_disagreement: The paper disagrees with previous findings by Dai et al. (2021) regarding the generalization capability of ViT variants, stating that unlike those variants, MobileViT shows better generalization capability.

a_title: Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models
b_title: Yi: Open Foundation Models by 01.AI
summary_of_disagreement: The paper strongly disagrees with the quantity-scaling styled instruction tuning approaches used by FLAN and UltraChat, criticizing their data-intensive methods for not carefully examining each data entry due to the large scale.

a_title: NeRF in the Wild: Neural Radiance Fields for Unconstrained Photo Collections
b_title: NeRV: Neural Reflectance and Visibility Fields for Relighting and View Synthesis
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses a strong disagreement with the approach used by "NeRF in the Wild", specifically criticizing its inability to generalize and simulate lighting conditions that were not seen during training.

a_title: StyleCLIP: Text-Driven Manipulation of StyleGAN Imagery
b_title: Imagic: Text-Based Real Image Editing with Diffusion Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses strong disagreement with the current leading methods in image manipulation using GANs, highlighting their limitations such as being restricted to specific edits, operating only on certain image domains, or requiring auxiliary inputs.

a_title: Encoding in Style: a StyleGAN Encoder for Image-to-Image Translation
b_title: DiffusionCLIP: Text-Guided Diffusion Models for Robust Image Manipulation
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses strong disagreement with the performance of state-of-the-art (SOTA) encoder-based GAN inversion approaches, including pSp [32], e4e [40], and ReStyle [3], criticizing their inability to reconstruct images with novel poses, views, and details effectively.

a_title: Efficient Geometry-aware 3D Generative Adversarial Networks
b_title: K-Planes: Explicit Radiance Fields in Space, Time, and Appearance
summary_of_disagreement: The paper expresses a clear disagreement with prior work on tri-plane models [5, 26] regarding the method of combining features. While previous models added features together, this paper advocates for multiplying them to maintain competitive reconstruction quality and enable a white-box model approach.

a_title: A Multitask, Multilingual, Multimodal Evaluation of ChatGPT on Reasoning, Hallucination, and Interactivity
b_title: Summary of ChatGPT-Related Research and Perspective Towards the Future of Large Language Models
summary_of_disagreement: The paper shows disagreement with the technical performance of ChatGPT in handling non-textual semantic reasoning tasks, highlighting its poor performance in multi-hop reasoning.
